title: Mecha Trace
problem: Mecha has a state of the art model and 17,000 interpretable features (interpretability blog), but the AmeriRad rollout will not be gated on CheXbert F1 - it will be gated on PACS/DICOM ingestion at line rate and HL7 report write back without surprises, which is the load bearing operational layer they have not yet publicly shown. More importantly: when a radiologist disagrees with a draft, today's pipeline gives them a paragraph to edit.
direction: A feature attributed report review SDK and viewer: every sentence in a Mecha draft links to a named SAE feature, a saliency overlay on the image, and a one click accept/reject that re renders the report and writes an audit row to a verifiable log.
why: The judgment is choosing the interpretability surface, not a new training run. Mecha's competitive moat is already the model; what they don't yet have is the clinician facing artifact that turns interpretability into a regulatory and adoption advantage.

archetypes.name: mecha evidence replay
archetypes.trigger: mecha signal changes while state context is stale
archetypes.expected: block release until cited evidence is regenerated

archetypes.name: state boundary probe
archetypes.trigger: state handoff crosses a policy or trust boundary
archetypes.expected: route to reviewer with evidence packet

archetypes.name: model regression harness
archetypes.trigger: model behavior regresses against the last accepted fixture
archetypes.expected: open a regression issue with trace and benchmark delta

archetypes.name: interpretable operator packet
archetypes.trigger: interpretable output needs a human-readable audit packet
archetypes.expected: accept only if decision claims cite fixture evidence
